target.name: Percepto
target.founder: Dor Abuhasira, Ariel Avitan, Sagi Blonder, Raviv Raz

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $128M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Tekever
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series C)

private.name: Monarch Tractor
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series C)

data.narrative:
Percepto is a Series C autonomous systems company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, having raised $128M to date. The relevant peer universe consists of 6 private companies in the same sector, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, and Archer Aviation, with no direct public comparables available.

Stage-progression valuation is the primary applicable method here. Using total raised of $128M as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement yields an implied valuation of $218M. A peer-median approach could not be reliably applied due to insufficient granular comparability: while four same-sector peers exist, fewer than three qualify as true same-stage or adjacent-stage matches with similar valuation tiers, making sector-wide median comparison potentially misleading.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $185M to $250M, with a median of $218M. This range reflects a ±15% sensitivity band around the primary stage-progression method. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 1.7x multiplier for Percepto's specific sub-segment within Drones & Autonomous, the representativeness of total raised as a current valuation proxy absent explicit interim rounds, and Percepto's relative positioning versus the fragmented peer set on unit economics, customer traction, and go-to-market differentiation.
